canonical_question: What OAuth 2.0 flows does Salesforce support and which should I use for server-to-server integration?
aliases: Salesforce OAuth 2.0 JWT bearer flow server-to-server
Salesforce client credentials flow vs JWT bearer flow
Salesforce connected app OAuth authentication setup
Salesforce External Client App OAuth configuration Spring 2026

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Spring '26 (v66.0) -- Connected App creation disabled by default; must use External Client Apps for new integrations

constraints: Connected App creation disabled by default in Spring '26 -- new integrations must use External Client Apps or admin must re-enable Connected Apps
JWT Bearer flow requires X.509 digital certificate uploaded to Connected App/External Client App
Client Credentials flow always runs as a designated execution user -- no per-user context
JWT assertion exp claim must be within 5 minutes of current time -- clock skew beyond 5 min causes authentication failure
OAuth 2.0 Device Flow permanently removed September 2025 -- no exceptions or extensions
Refresh tokens default to 'valid until revoked' but admin can set inactivity timeout as short as 15 minutes
Username-Password flow incompatible with MFA enforcement -- breaks when org enables mandatory MFA

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs to understand API rate limits and governor limits
User needs bulk data migration authentication patterns
User needs SSO/SAML for end-user login (not API integration)

related_to.label: Salesforce Composite API -- uses OAuth tokens from these flows
Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 -- uses same authentication flows

solves.label: Salesforce REST API -- requires OAuth authentication covered here

often_confused_with.label: SSO/SAML is for end-user login; OAuth is for API integration

inputs_needed.question: What integration pattern do you need?
Do API calls need to run as a specific end-user or a service account?
Can your organization manage X.509 certificates?
options: yes -- we can generate, store, and rotate certificates
no -- we need a simpler credential model
